titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in the Book of Mulling
VL35
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Mc Lc Jo

layout.p: In two columns, with one page towards the end of John in three columns.

surrogates.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.
Colour digital images on CD in ITSEE supplied by TCD.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers ff. 82r to 94r.The transcription includes foliation, lineation and all punctuation. Most abbreviations have been silently expanded. Occasional cases of ambiguity are mentioned in the notes. The scribe has two ways of writing the letter 'a', which have not been distinguished. The consistency of the writing and the left-hand margin are very uneven: indications of hanging lines are approximate. A corrector has added Eusebian canons and reinforced the text at the beginning of each canon, adding punctuation: these have not been recorded. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Change to verse division of 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: 'et habitauit' is written by the first hand in blank space on the line above, which reads 'nati sunt // et habitauit'. Future instances of this will not be noted.
Reinking by second hand with addition of Ammonian section.
'ante' written 'an¬'.
Hole in parchment means that the next six lines are indented to the right.
As on the recto, there is a hole in the parchment over the next six lines.
The correction is added on the far side of the whole. Although the ink colour is darker, it could be first hand.
the first example of the '7 ' abbreviation for 'et ' - there are more in the next column
It is possible that the corrector added 'illum' at the end of the previous line.
abbreviation is just si
This is written as an abbreviation, 'dc¬s', presumably in error for 'dn¬s'.
The 'tu¬' of 'istu¬' is written at the end of the line above, after 'mala'.
'sim scitis' has been written by the first hand in the space at the end of the line above.
possibly corrected from ad hos
About half the line is blank: this could be an erasure.
eu¬ is written above the line by the first hand.
The word 'ih¬s' is written in blank space at the end of the line above.
The addition in the right margin is not completely legible.
sic
The words 'dicit eis ih¬s' are added in the blank space at the end of the previous line.
sic
sic
There is no obvious reason for this gap.
The words 'scio quia cum' are written in blank space at the end of the line above.
sic
There is a space between the 'm' and 'o' of 'salemonis' which may be a flaw in the parchment.
The 'nia' of 'bethania' is written in the space at the end of the preceding line.
sic
sic

The amount of missing text requires the positing of this line. However, the next line, ending 'traditurus', is level with the first line of the second column, and it is possible that some of the text of this verse was omitted: the traces of text visible above this column may be part of the correction to John 12:27-29 in column 2.
It is not clear whether this correction over the top of the word also indicates the dittography in the following verse.
The correction has been added at the end of the previous line, and 'abit' cancelled.

rdg.note: This part of the correction, in the top margin of the page, is no longer extant.
This part of the correction, in the top margin of the page, is no longer extant.
This part of the correction, in the top margin of the page, is no longer extant.

ab.note: The correction is added at the end of the previous line.
The first three lines of this page appear to be indented, perhaps because of damage to the parchment.
The 'em' of 'patrem' is written on the line above.
From 'et possui' to 'patrem' is written by the first hand in rasura.
There are gaps in the middle of the word "f ui ssem", apparently because of repair to the parchment.
The missing text is added at the foot of the page.
The missing text is added at the foot of the page.
The missing text is added at the foot of the page.
The 'in' is slightly outside the level of the column and may be an addition.
Possible correction from 'tollit'.

The scribe appears a) to have written 'a mondo'; b) to have changed this to 'de mondo'; then c) to have written 'a malo' after 'de mondo', with a symbol indicating the two phrases should be transposed.
The abbreviation 'tm¬' could also represent 'tamen', although the scribe seems to use 'tn¬' for this (see f. 94r).
The digital images are unclear and there may be traces of a correction with 'si' above the line.

ab.w:
habe
The format of the manuscript changes from two columns to three columns.

ab.note: 'dicit' and 'tho' possibly written in rasura.
The correction 'esset' is written in the line below, underneath 'est', even though the orginal 'est' does not appear to have been cancelled.
Lawlor p. 37 omits 'est', but there is a horizontal line visible in keeping with the abbreviation for 'est' despite the tear in the parchment.

The subscription continues on at least six lines of the second column, ending '[nom]en autem scriptoris mulling dicitur . . Finiunt quatuor euangelia'. There is also writing by the first hand in the first column of folio 94v (a 'liturgical fragment' described in Lawlor 145-166), and a circular device on the bottom half of the page (see Lawlor 167-185).
